title: Shri Somnath Temple, Gujarat
intro: The Shri Somnath Temple, located in the Gir Somnath district of Gujarat, is one of the most ancient and sacred temples in India dedicated to Lord Shiva. It is famous as the first of the 12 Jyotirlingas of Lord Shiva.
description:
Shri Somnath Temple – Gujarat
Introduction
The Shri Somnath Temple, located in the Gir Somnath district of Gujarat, is one of the most ancient and sacred temples in India dedicated to Lord Shiva. It is famous as the first of the 12 Jyotirlingas of Lord Shiva.
Situated on the shores of the Arabian Sea, the Somnath Temple is world-renowned for its spiritual significance, ancient history, and grand architecture. Millions of devotees visit here every year for the darshan of Lord Somnath.
Temple Information
 Temple Name: Shri Somnath Temple
 Other Names: Somnath Jyotirlinga, Prabhas Kshetra Temple
 Location: Somnath Temple Road, Prabhas Patan, Gir Somnath District, Gujarat 362268, India
 Presiding Deity: Lord Shiva (First Jyotirlinga)
 Significance: First among the 12 Jyotirlingas
 Nearest Major City: Rajkot (approx. 195 km)
History and Significance
The Somnath Temple is considered one of the most ancient Shiva temples in India. According to religious beliefs, Lord Shiva resides here in the form of a Jyotirlinga.
The Somnath region is mentioned specially in the Puranas. This place is also famous as the Prabhas Kshetra.
The temple has been reconstructed several times in history, but its religious faith and importance have always remained intact. The present temple is a symbol of Indian architecture and spiritual heritage.
How to Reach
✈️ By Air
 Diu Airport: Distance from Somnath Temple is approx. 85 km.
 Rajkot International Airport: Distance from Somnath Temple is approx. 195 km.
 Taxis and buses are available from the airport to reach the temple.
🚆 By Train
 Veraval Railway Station: Distance from the temple is approx. 7 km. Taxis, autos, and local transport are easily available from Veraval.
🛣️ By Road
Somnath is well-connected by road to Rajkot, Ahmedabad, Junagadh, and Diu. You can reach here by bus, taxi, or private vehicle.
Best Time to Visit
For Somnath Yatra:
October to March is considered the best time as the weather remains pleasant during this period.
Special Occasions:
 Mahashivratri
 Kartik Purnima
 Shravan Maas Utsav
Temple Timings
🕒 6:00 AM to 10:00 PM
(Timings may change during special festivals and religious programs.)
Main Festivals
 Mahashivratri: A major festival for the special worship and celebration of Lord Shiva.
 Kartik Purnima: An occasion of special religious significance in the Somnath region.
 Shravan Maas Utsav: Special worship of Lord Shiva is performed during the month of Shravan.
Nearby Attractions
 Triveni Sangam: A place of religious importance where three holy rivers meet.
 Bhalka Tirth: A sacred place believed to be associated with Lord Shri Krishna.
 Somnath Beach: A beautiful location on the coast of the Arabian Sea.
 Prabhas Patan Museum: A center for information related to the history and archaeology of the region.
Dress Code
To visit the temple:
 Wearing modest and traditional clothing is considered appropriate.
 Devotees should maintain respectful attire in accordance with the religious environment.
Facilities for Devotees
 Darshan arrangements
 Security arrangements
 Prasad facilities
 Help centers
 Nearby hotels and Dharmashalas (guesthouses)
 Local transport facilities
Conclusion
The Shri Somnath Temple is one of the holiest Jyotirlingas of Lord Shiva. Located on the coast of the Arabian Sea, this temple is a symbol of India's ancient religious tradition, faith, and culture. A pilgrimage to Somnath is a special opportunity for devotees to attain faith, peace, and a spiritual experience of Lord Shiva.

faq.q1: To which deity is the Somnath Temple dedicated?
faq.a1: The Somnath Temple is dedicated to Lord Shiva and is considered the first Jyotirlinga.
faq.q2: Where is the Somnath Temple located?
faq.a2: It is located in Prabhas Patan, Gir Somnath district, Gujarat.
faq.q3: Which is the nearest railway station to the Somnath Temple?
faq.q4: What is the best time to visit the Somnath Temple?
faq.a4: The period from October to March is considered good for the pilgrimage.
faq.q5: Which places can one visit near the Somnath Temple?
faq.a5: Triveni Sangam, Bhalka Tirth, Somnath Beach, and Prabhas Patan Museum are the major ones.
